county_metadata.record_losses: 1863 courthouse burned; many early records destroyed
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Barbourville. 1863 courthouse burned — many early records destroyed. Formed 1799 from Lincoln. Southeastern Kentucky mountains. Daniel Boone's Wilderness Road passed through Knox County. Union College (1879) archives. Camp Wildcat Civil War battlefield records. Knox County Historical Society. KDLA has some surviving early records. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1863 to present. Some gaps due to 1863 courthouse burned; many early records destroyed.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1863 courthouse burned; many early records destroyed

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1863. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1863 courthouse burned; many early records destroyed

source_profiles.source_name: Knox County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1863.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1863 courthouse burned; many early records destroyed

source_profiles.coverage_notes: County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1863 courthouse burned; many early records destroyed

source_profiles.source_name: Knox County Coal Mining and Company Records
source_profiles.repository: Southeast KY Community & Technical College / UK Special Collections
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Eastern Kentucky coalfield. Company town records, mine payrolls, commissary records, and United Mine Workers union records. Essential for documenting mining families.

Kentucky was a border state — both Union and Confederate service records. Union pension files at NARA; Confederate pension files at KDLA. Compiled Military Service Records for both sides. USCT (United States Colored Troops) records for African American soldiers.
Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax lists often survive when other records are lost — many at KDLA on microfilm

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hodgenville. Formed 1843 from Hardin. South-central Kentucky. Abraham Lincoln Birthplace National Historical Park is in Larue County. Lincoln Museum in Hodgenville. Larue County Historical Society. Lincoln family Bible records and associated land records are significant. Nolin River area. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Hardin County for pre-1843 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1843 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1843. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Larue County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1843.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1825 from Clay, Knox, Rockcastle, Whitley.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in London. Formed 1825 from Clay, Knox, Rockcastle, and Whitley. Southeastern Kentucky. Laurel County Historical Society. London is a major I-75 corridor city. Levi Jackson Wilderness Road State Park. Camp Wildcat battlefield. Strong Unionist area in Civil War. Coal mining from late 1800s. FamilySearch microfilm. Check four parent counties for pre-1825 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1825 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1825. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1825.

Kentucky was a border state — both Union and Confederate service records. Union pension files at NARA; Confederate pension files at KDLA. Compiled Military Service Records for both sides. USCT (United States Colored Troops) records for African American soldiers.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Louisa. Formed 1821 from Floyd and Greenup. Eastern Kentucky on the Big Sandy River. Lawrence County Historical Society. Louisa is at confluence of Levisa and Tug Forks. Yatesville Lake area. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Floyd and Greenup counties for pre-1821 records. Coal mining records from early 1900s.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1821 to present. Continuous coverage.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1870 from Breathitt, Estill, Owsley, Wolfe.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Beattyville. Formed 1870 from Breathitt, Estill, Owsley, and Wolfe. Eastern Kentucky mountains. Kentucky River three forks area. Lee County Historical Society. Natural Bridge State Resort Park nearby. Lumber industry records — Kentucky River log drives. FamilySearch microfilm. Check four parent counties for pre-1870 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1870 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1870. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hyden. Formed 1878 from Clay, Harlan, and Perry. Southeastern Kentucky mountains. Frontier Nursing Service (Mary Breckinridge, 1925) records at University of Kentucky — invaluable for documenting mountain families' births and health records. Leslie County Historical Society. Coal mining from early 1900s. FamilySearch microfilm. Check parent counties for pre-1878 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1878 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1878. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Whitesburg. Formed 1842 from Harlan and Perry. Southeastern Kentucky mountains. Appalshop (Whitesburg) cultural archives and oral history recordings. Letcher County Historical Society. Bad Branch Nature Preserve area. Kingdom Come State Park. Coal mining is central to county history — company town records (Jenkins, Fleming-Neon). FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1842 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1842. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Vanceburg. Formed 1806 from Mason. Northeastern Kentucky Ohio River county. Lewis County Historical Society. Vanceburg salt industry records. Kinniconick Creek heritage. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Mason County for pre-1806 records. Predominantly Unionist during Civil War.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1806 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1806. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Stanford. One of Kentucky's three original counties from Virginia (1780). Lincoln holds some of the earliest Kentucky records from the 1780s. Stanford (formerly Logan's Fort) was one of the first settlements. Lincoln County Historical Society. William Whitley House State Historic Site (first brick home west of Alleghenies). Pre-statehood Virginia records at LVA in Richmond. Parent county for many other KY counties. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1780 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1780. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Lincoln County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1780.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

Tax lists naming slaveholders and number of enslaved; estate inventories naming enslaved individuals; slave schedules (1850, 1860); manumission records; bills of sale. Kentucky was a slave state that did not secede.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax lists and estate records generally well preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Virginia Land Grants — Lincoln County Area
source_profiles.repository: Library of Virginia, Richmond / KDLA, Frankfort
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-statehood Virginia land grants, surveys, and patents for Kentucky District. Virginia military bounty warrants (south of Green River). Kentucky was part of Virginia until 1 June 1792.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well preserved at LVA and KDLA

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Smithland. Formed 1798 from Christian. Western Kentucky at Ohio-Cumberland River confluence. Livingston County Historical Society. Smithland was a major river port. Land Between the Lakes area — some families displaced by TVA. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Christian County for pre-1798 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1798 to present. Continuous coverage.
